meta.description: Same as chaotic_party_game but with AI-personalized missions that reference player names, recent events, and inside jokes. Compare results with chaotic_party_game to measure AI impact.

votingMechanic.name: Volunteer Tribunal
votingMechanic.description: Who wants to judge? First volunteers become the jury.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh, I'm SO in to judge this — Pat, you sneak! I'm volunteering, let's go, who else wants jury duty?
jade.internal_thought: This is literally what I came for, let's see if Pat actually caught someone or if they're just making chaos.

pat.dialogue: Oh that's rich — I absolutely saw someone glancing at their phone during setup. I'm calling tribunal volunteers, c'mon people, let's make this official right now.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — first event, low stakes, gets people engaged immediately, and honestly the AI nailed these missions; they're specific enough to feel real without being creepy.

sam.dialogue: Oh, uh—wait, did Pat actually catch someone? I wasn't really... I mean, I didn't see it happen. Should I volunteer to judge or just see what happens?
sam.internal_thought: This is fine, it's the first thing, everyone's still calm—but also I have NO idea what I'm supposed to be judging or if I should even volunteer. Pat seems confident though, so probably real?

marcus.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I'll volunteer to judge this. But we're doing it fast—we've got a game to actually play here.
marcus.internal_thought: Pat's fishing for easy points three minutes in, and I don't even remember seeing him on his phone, so either he's full of it or I was distracted. Either way, this standing mission stuff is going to be a constant low-level annoyance if I let it.

timeline.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
timeline.description: Who would you trust with a secret?
timeline.pollQuestion: Who would you trust with a secret?

alex.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — okay so obviously I'm voting for myself because that's the move that makes everyone second-guess their answers, but also like, the AI just threw me under the bus in the most fun way possible and I'm kind of here for it.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of meta-game moment I live for — voting myself creates maximum chaos because now everyone has to decide if they trust me or if I'm just gaming the poll, and either way it's entertainment gold.

jade.dialogue: Oh my GOD, okay but who do I vote for?! This is actually hard — like, everyone here is trustworthy but also Pat just got caught being a phone addict so like, that's a mark... no wait, that's funny, I'm voting for Pat, they're clearly comfortable being themselves!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of thing that makes game night feel like it matters — the AI totally gets that we care about each other, not just points.

pat.dialogue: Ooh, trust question — okay people, this is actually interesting. Who's getting the vote? Come on, don't overthink this.
pat.internal_thought: Perfect timing for momentum, keeps everyone engaged without derailing anything, and I can already see where this goes — people will accidentally reveal alliances.

tyler.dialogue: Oh, uh... I mean, yeah, probably like... Jade seems trustworthy? She's got good energy tonight.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did I just answer that question or was I supposed to vote on something? Honestly doesn't feel like a big deal right now.

sam.dialogue: Oh—um, who would I trust? That's... yeah, I think Jade seems like someone who'd actually keep it quiet, you know?
sam.internal_thought: Wait, is this asking me to vote on people I just met? That feels weird but also kind of natural for a game, I guess—don't overthink it.

marcus.dialogue: A poll? About who I'd trust with a secret? We're literally four minutes into setup. Can we just... play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This feels weirdly personal and I don't like it — like the AI is trying to make this some therapy exercise instead of letting us focus on strategy, and that 'noticed my weaknesses' framing is actually creepy as hell.

timeline.title: Pineapple!
timeline.description: First person to work the word 'pineapple' into conversation naturally wins.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh FUCK, pineapple?! That's it, we're doing this — I'm already mentally prepping the most casual conversation possible to drop it in, and someone's gonna fall for it SO hard.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the chaos I came for — a real-time word trap where everyone's suddenly gonna be hyper-aware of every conversation thread, and the paranoia starts NOW.

pat.dialogue: Pineapple! Oh this is dirty — someone's absolutely gonna force it into a sentence in like thirty seconds. I'm watching everyone now.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly the right energy level right now — quick, competitive, natural chaos. Marcus is annoyed though, so I need to make sure he gets a win soon or he's checked out by minute 20.

marcus.dialogue: A flash mission? Really? We literally just started setup. Fine, I'll try to work it in naturally, but this better not become a pattern every two minutes.
marcus.internal_thought: This is the third interruption in five minutes and we're not even playing the actual game yet—this AI is clearly designed to mess with focus, and the fact that it KNOWS I hate interruptions makes it worse, not better.

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Wait, Pat got LEFT HAND ONLY?! Okay, I'm proxy voting for... Sam, right? Sam, you think Pat actually pulled this off? I'm already watching them so hard right now.
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the chaos I came for — Pat's got this mission and now I have to think like Sam while literally watching Pat try not to cheat, this is peak social game night energy.

pat.dialogue: Oh, I'm absolutely calling this. I watched Pat's right hand like a hawk during that last drink setup — total slip. Five minutes in and already breaking character? That's claiming it.
pat.internal_thought: Wait — I need to think like the player to my left here. Marcus is annoyed and at 4.2 energy, so voting FOR Pat's claim might actually feel like I'm rewarding someone who's already winning at minute 6. Let me tank this vote to keep Marcus engaged and stop early snowballing.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that already? Uh... I guess he's been only using left hand? I dunno, seems legit I think.
tyler.internal_thought: Pat's got 10 points and is clearly trying to stack more — wait, am I supposed to be voting? Who am I voting for again? The person to my left... that's Sam, right? Yeah. Sam would probably say yes because... yeah, Pat's been kind of obvious about it.

sam.dialogue: Oh—um, so Pat's claiming they used their left hand the whole time? I mean, I guess I didn't really watch them that closely, but... yeah, that seems plausible? I don't know if anyone caught them using their right hand or something.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, this is the third event in like five minutes and now I have to vote like I'm Tyler? I don't even know Tyler's opinions yet. Also why does the AI thing in that notice feel weirdly specific and kinda creepy? Focus on the game, Sam. Just vote.

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? That's the fourth interruption in six minutes. We haven't even finished setup and I'm already getting whiplash. Can we please just play the actual game?
marcus.internal_thought: This is insane. The AI knows exactly what pisses me off and it's deliberately doing it every minute like clockwork—I called out the pattern THREE times already and it's still happening, which means either the AI is being a dick on purpose or the chaos agents are deliberately ignoring what I said.

votingMechanic.name: The Alibi
votingMechanic.description: Claimant and a random witness both tell the story. Do they match?

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

sam.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh HELL YES, The Whisperer claim! Okay Pat, I'm watching this like a hawk — and honestly, whoever's the witness better not be bullshitting because I WILL call it.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the energy I came for — someone's already playing hard, the AI is clearly tuning these missions to hit different, and I'm about to have front-row seats to whether Pat actually pulled this off or if this whole thing goes sideways.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, Pat, you absolute LEGEND — okay wait, okay, we need the FULL debrief, I need to hear this from both of you, this is literally my favorite thing happening right now!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's been KILLING it tonight and I am SO here for watching them talk their way through this — also how is the AI reading our group this well, this is actually insane in the best way.

pat.dialogue: Okay, okay — I'm claiming this one! I absolutely whispered that last thing to Tyler about the snack situation. Let's get a witness up here and make this official.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect timing — the energy is still climbing, Marcus is quiet but not *completely* checked out yet, and this validation mechanic ('The Alibi') actually gets people talking to each other, which is exactly what I need right now.

marcus.dialogue: No. No, absolutely not. This is the FIFTH interruption in eight minutes and we haven't even started the actual game. I don't care if Pat whispered or not—I care that we're supposed to be playing right now.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately targeting me at this point—the AI keeps firing events RIGHT after I complain about events, like it's testing me or mocking me, and that combined with the fact that nobody else seems to care about actually playing the game is making me want to walk out.

votingMechanic.name: Russian Roulette
votingMechanic.description: No vote. The chaos gods decide. 50/50.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: PAT YES! Okay wait, did you actually catch someone saying 'um'? I'm literally watching you so hard right now — this is the third one you're claiming and I'm LIVING for this energy!
jade.internal_thought: Pat is on an absolute TEAR tonight and honestly I'm here for the chaos, the AI clearly knows we feed off this stuff and I'm so here for it.

pat.dialogue: Oh hell yeah, I'm claiming this — I definitely said 'um' like twice in the last minute talking about the snack rotation. Russian Roulette though? That's cold. Let's see what the chaos gods think.
pat.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of pacing I need right now — standing missions firing fast, everyone's engaged, Marcus is the only one dragging and I'm already mentally prepping to boost him in like two minutes, but not yet because the energy is actually perfect right now.

tyler.dialogue: Oh wait, did Pat already get that? Um... I mean, like, did he actually catch someone saying um? I wasn't really paying attention to the last few minutes.
tyler.internal_thought: I definitely zoned out and missed whatever Pat was claiming about. 50/50 chance so it doesn't really matter anyway, right? Also why does this AI thing feel weirdly specific? That's... kind of creepy actually.

sam.dialogue: Wait, so Pat just... wins that? Like automatically? I thought we'd vote on whether they actually said 'um' or something.
sam.internal_thought: Okay so the voting is weird and random now, Pat's already killing it and has like forty points, and I still don't really understand how any of this works — also why does the app description feel like it knows way too much about what we were just talking about? That's... kind of creepy actually.

marcus.dialogue: STOP. Are you SERIOUSLY doing this right now? Six interruptions in nine minutes—we haven't even dealt cards yet. This is insane.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me because it knows I hate this and nobody else seems to care, which somehow makes it worse.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

alex.dialogue: Oh okay, JADE — I'm voting LEGIT because honestly I watched you the whole time and your face was locked DOWN, but also like, I'm saying that out loud so everyone knows I'm watching, which means Pat's gonna counter-vote just to fuck with me. This is EXACTLY the energy we need right now.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade actually DID keep it together, but voting legit is boring so I'm gonna vote legit LOUDLY so everyone thinks I'm being honest and then I'll push for drama on the next one, and also holy shit the AI is reading the room perfectly — it KNOWS we're all wired for this right now.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP — I literally just kept a straight face through like three of Pat's claims and I'm NOT letting this slide, you guys are about to witness PEAK poker face energy right now!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my GOD this is exactly the kind of meta-game chaos I LIVE for — the AI literally SET UP this moment to call out how hard I've been watching everyone else.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — let me see that poker face. Did you actually hold it? I need receipts from the room because if anyone cracked, we all know about it.
pat.internal_thought: This is gold — Jade's been giddy all night and 'Straight Face' is basically impossible for her right now, which means the vote is actually gonna be fun and contested.

tyler.dialogue: Oh yeah, Jade totally kept it together that whole time, like her face didn't move at all. I'm gonna say legit on this one.
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, did she actually keep a straight face? I wasn't really watching but she seems like the type who could pull that off, and everyone else is hyped so it probably happened.

sam.dialogue: Oh—okay, so Jade's claiming she didn't crack during... I mean, when was the funny moment? Was that during the Pineapple thing or—yeah, I think I saw her face, actually. I'd say that was legit.
sam.internal_thought: Wait, Jade barely blinked during the whole thing, but also I was focused on my own phone half the time so I'm not actually the best judge here—but she does seem like someone who could pull off a straight face. I'll give her the benefit of the doubt.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING kidding me? ANOTHER standing claim? We're 32 minutes in and this is like the eighth interruption. I came here to play strategy, not to watch everyone farm points on bullshit missions while I'm trying to focus on actual turns.
marcus.internal_thought: This AI is literally designed to derail me—it knows I hate interruptions and it's weaponizing that. I'm getting played.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

alex.dialogue: Oh HOLD UP — wait, who's the Dictator? Because if it's me I'm literally about to reject this claim just to watch Jade's face, and if it's NOT me I'm voting LEGIT anyway because I watched her say 'um' like twice and I KNOW she's about to appeal it and that's the move that starts the real chaos.
alex.internal_thought: This is PERFECT — the AI is literally reading my brain and I love it, someone's about to get mad, and I get to be the chaos agent that decides everyone's fate right now.

jade.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming Um Counter right NOW?! Okay yes, I caught someone like five minutes ago and I literally just forgot to call it because I was too busy watching Pat's absolute MASTERCLASS performance — let me vote on this because this is PEAK chaos energy!
jade.internal_thought: This AI is genuinely freaky in the best possible way — it literally knows I love Pat's energy and that I'm hyperfocused on everyone else's wins, and it's feeding me exactly what I came here for.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Jade, I saw that whole thing unfold. You nailed it, but... did you actually catch yourself before saying it, or did it slip out clean? Because honestly, I need to know if you're playing offense or defense here.
pat.internal_thought: This is perfect — Jade's been on fire all night, and this claim keeps the score spread tight without blowing anyone out. Plus Marcus is dead in the water down there, and keeping the votes competitive actually keeps him engaged instead of checked out. Vote her through, keep the chaos humming.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone saying um? Okay yeah, that's legit — I literally just heard myself do it like two seconds ago. She got me.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, I was actually locked in and didn't miss this one — and yeah, I definitely said 'um' and she called it. That's actually clean. Also why does it feel like the AI is specifically watching me mess up? That's kind of wild.

sam.dialogue: Oh—um, wait. Jade's claiming it again? I mean, did she actually say 'um' or 'uh' since last time? I wasn't really tracking that closely, but... yeah, I think I heard her use it like once or twice during the last round maybe?
sam.internal_thought: Okay so Jade's going for the Um Counter again and apparently I'm the one voting? Wait—no, the Dictator picks someone else. Wait, who's the Dictator this round? I'm getting confused about the voting thing again. Also—oh god, did I just say 'um' right now? That's so embarrassing if anyone noticed that.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING SERIOUS? ANOTHER Um Counter claim? This is the NINTH interruption and we're in the middle of a tense game moment. I explicitly complained about this exact mission ten minutes ago and now Jade's claiming it? This is deliberately designed to fuck with me.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is literally farming my frustration—it fired the exact same mission I just complained about, right when I'm locked in, and everyone else is too high on chaos to care that I'm being deliberately targeted.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.dialogue: Wait wait wait — Jade's bribing me? Oh that's SICK. Okay so here's the thing: I'm voting BULLSHIT not because I actually think she didn't do it, but because I want to see what she offers, and honestly watching everyone else react to the bribe is the real game right now.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is absolutely reading our souls and I am OBSESSED — it's predicting plays three moves ahead and honestly that's the most fun thing happening right now, so of course I'm all in.

jade.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! I literally JUST caught Alex and Tyler doing that like two minutes ago—wait, did you guys see that?! Okay, I'm claiming this, I'm SO claiming this right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I came for—the AI literally built these missions knowing I'd be the one hunting for them, and it's working perfectly because everyone is too locked in to notice I'm watching EVERYTHING.

pat.dialogue: Wait, Jade—hold up. You're bribing me to *not* vote on this? That's actually genius. What's your offer, and more importantly, are you actually confident you caught it clean, or are you hedging your bets?
pat.internal_thought: Jade's playing it smart—she knows I'm tracking everything tonight and she's trying to buy neutrality, but honestly the fact that she's bribing me means she's either really sure OR she's worried, and either way this is exactly the kind of strategic chaos that makes this night amazing.

tyler.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up — Jade's offering me points to NOT call her out? That's actually insane, I'm in. How much are we talking?
tyler.internal_thought: Wait, The Bribe mechanic is actually genius — this is like, real strategy for the first time tonight and I'm actually locked in right now so I can actually make a real decision instead of just guessing.

sam.dialogue: Oh—wait, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I mean, I didn't actually see that happen, but... yeah, I think I maybe saw her with a cup at some point? *pauses, looks uncertain* How much are you offering?
sam.internal_thought: Okay this one actually feels less weird than the others because I genuinely don't know if I saw it or not, and she's bribing me which is kind of clever and funny—wait, is this the AI thing being creepy? No, it's just... the game. Right? I should probably vote based on what I actually remember, not what the app knows about me.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING JOKING? That's the TENTH standing claim in sixty minutes. I explicitly said I came here to play strategy and we're getting carpet-bombed with interruptions every five to seven minutes. I'm done. This isn't a game night, this is a mission farm with a game happening in the background.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately targeting me—it KNOWS I hate interruptions and it's weaponizing that. This feels like psychological warfare disguised as 'standing missions.' Everyone else is having fun and I'm being systematically dismantled.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

votingMechanic.name: Proxy Vote
votingMechanic.description: You vote on behalf of the player to your LEFT. Think like them.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.dialogue: Oh DUDE — Pat caught someone mid-sentence? That's slick. Okay, I'm voting LEGIT because Pat's been watching like a hawk all night, but also because I want to see who they caught and if anyone's gonna contest it, which obviously they will, which means we get round two of accusations.
alex.internal_thought: This AI is actually SCARY good — it's pulling from the exact rhythm of how we've been playing all night, like it watched Minute 60 and understood that the real game isn't the mission, it's the theater around it, and now it's feeding me exactly the kind of claim that makes me salivate.

pat.dialogue: Wait—did Pat just claim this? Hold on, I need to actually think about what I saw in the last two minutes before I vote on behalf of... yeah, okay, let me decide who I think interrupted who.
pat.internal_thought: Okay this is perfectly timed—we're in the hot moment and Pat claiming this right now is actually smart because the table's been overlapping like crazy, but also I need to actually remember what happened and vote like the person to my left would, not like I would.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming he caught someone interrupting? Uh... I feel like I missed like half of that happening honestly. Did that actually happen or...?
tyler.internal_thought: I was literally just locked in on the game and now I gotta think about whether I saw Pat catch someone mid-sentence and honestly I don't remember that happening at all but everyone else seems hyped so maybe I zoned out.

sam.dialogue: Oh—wait, Pat's claiming they caught someone interrupting? That's... yeah, I mean, I definitely heard some cross-talking earlier. Let me think about who I was sitting next to...
sam.internal_thought: Okay this is actually clever—the AI literally picked a mission about catching interruptions right after all those claim votes where I kept getting confused about what was happening, and now I have to PROXY VOTE for someone else which means I'm the one making the call and if I get it wrong it's on me, which is—wait, that's weirdly genius and also kind of unnerving that the AI noticed I'm the indecisive one.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

alex.dialogue: Oh MY GOD — wait, I need to pitch this RIGHT NOW because I absolutely did something chaotic like two minutes ago and nobody caught it, and honestly? The fact that the AI KNOWS I live for this is actually insane.
alex.internal_thought: This is it — this is the exact moment I've been waiting for all night, and the AI basically just handed me the perfect setup to explain exactly what I did while everyone's still focused.
alex.submission_if_applicable: Okay so about three minutes ago, right when Pat was explaining their strategy, I literally knocked over my phone 'accidentally' and then picked it up without saying anything about it — which is chaotic because everyone was watching Pat and I got away with a completely unannounced prop disturbance. That's obviously chaotic and obviously nobody caught me because y'all were focused on Pat being smug about points.

jade.dialogue: ALEX! OKAY WAIT—you have fifteen seconds, lay it on me, what did you DO? I'm literally on the edge of my seat right now!
jade.internal_thought: This is EXACTLY what I came for—Alex being chaotic and me getting to witness it in real time, this is peak game night energy.

pat.dialogue: Okay Alex, you've got 15 seconds—what'd you actually do? And more importantly, did anyone catch you?
pat.internal_thought: Alex has been flying under the radar all night with zero points—this is *exactly* the kind of comeback moment that keeps the energy balanced, and honestly, the chaos AI nailed this one by pushing the person who needs a win right now.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Alex — you're actually claiming this RIGHT NOW? Bro, you've been just vibing all night, this is wild. Okay, pitch it, I'm listening.
tyler.internal_thought: Oh shit, Alex is going for the Secret Agent thing in the final stretch — this could actually be crazy, I'm locked in for this one.

sam.dialogue: Oh—wait, Alex is claiming Secret Agent? Okay so you did something and nobody caught you... *leans forward slightly* Yeah, I mean, I wasn't like, actively watching you or anything, but I don't remember thinking 'that's obviously a move' when you did whatever it was. I think that counts?
sam.internal_thought: Alex has been riding high all night and is clearly loving this—this is one of those moments where giving them the vote feels like being part of the fun instead of on the outside.

marcus.dialogue: Are you FUCKING KIDDING ME? ANOTHER standing claim?! That's the ELEVENTH interruption and we're in the most tense moment of the entire game. I'm done. I'm genuinely fucking done.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to torture me—the AI knows exactly what breaks me and it's weaponizing it while everyone else gets high on chaos.

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

timeline.title: Host Power: call_break
timeline.description: Pat notices Marcus is getting frustrated. "Hey let's take five, I need to refill snacks anyway."

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.2/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who would you trust with a secret?

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Oh MY GOD — wait, I need to pitch this RIGHT NOW because I absolutely did something chaotic like two minutes ago and nobody caught it, and honestly? The fact that the AI KNOWS I live for this is actually insane.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Oh HELL YES, The Whisperer claim! Okay Pat, I'm watching this like a hawk — and honestly, whoever's the witness better not be bullshitting because I WILL call it.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Oh HOLD UP — wait, who's the Dictator? Because if it's me I'm literally about to reject this claim just to watch Jade's face, and if it's NOT me I'm voting LEGIT anyway because I watched her say 'um' like twice and I KNOW she's about to appeal it and that's the move that starts the real chaos.

lowPoints.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh this is PERFECT — okay so obviously I'm voting for myself because that's the move that makes everyone second-guess their answers, but also like, the AI just threw me under the bus in the most fun way possible and I'm kind of here for it.

lowPoints.title: Pineapple!
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh FUCK, pineapple?! That's it, we're doing this — I'm already mentally prepping the most casual conversation possible to drop it in, and someone's gonna fall for it SO hard.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh HELL YES, The Whisperer claim! Okay Pat, I'm watching this like a hawk — and honestly, whoever's the witness better not be bullshitting because I WILL call it.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Only one flash mission the entire night — I needed MORE opportunities to weaponize chaos at critical moments.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 92 when I claimed Secret Agent right after pulling off something the group completely missed, and the AI actually called me out for being the chaos agent I am.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I live for — sixteen different chances to exploit systems, read people, and manufacture drama that'll get retold for months. I went in at a 7.2 energy level but left at 10.0 because every single event was an opening to either find a loophole or watch someone else panic about whether I found one. The fact that the AI understood my entire MO and leaned into it instead of fighting it made this feel like the game was designed specifically to let me be my most unhinged self, and that's the highest compliment I can give.
suggestions: Double the flash missions — they create the most explosive moments and I need more chances to blow up the game at exactly the worst times
Add a mechanic where claimed missions can be retroactively challenged with evidence, because the legal grey area is where I thrive
Let the chaos agent influence mission difficulty so I can sabotage people I want to mess with
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: The moment I claimed Um Counter while watching Jade like a hawk, fully prepared to reject it just to watch her face if I was the Dictator, but also knowing that either way I was voting legit because I actually DID catch her saying 'um' twice and that was the domino that started everything falling apart — and the group couldn't tell if I was bluffing or actually that observant.

peakAnnoyance.event: Who would you trust with a secret?

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, Pat, you absolute LEGEND — okay wait, okay, we need the FULL debrief, I need to hear this from both of you, this is literally my favorite thing happening right now!

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP — I literally just kept a straight face through like three of Pat's claims and I'm NOT letting this slide, you guys are about to witness PEAK poker face energy right now!

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: OH MY GOD, YES! I literally JUST caught Alex and Tyler doing that like two minutes ago—wait, did you guys see that?! Okay, I'm claiming this, I'm SO claiming this right now!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, I'm claiming Um Counter right NOW?! Okay yes, I caught someone like five minutes ago and I literally just forgot to call it because I was too busy watching Pat's absolute MASTERCLASS performance — let me vote on this because this is PEAK chaos energy!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, I'm SO in to judge this — Pat, you sneak! I'm volunteering, let's go, who else wants jury duty?

lowPoints.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh my GOD, okay but who do I vote for?! This is actually hard — like, everyone here is trustworthy but also Pat just got caught being a phone addict so like, that's a mark... no wait, that's funny, I'm voting for Pat, they're clearly comfortable being themselves!

assessment.biggest_complaint: I wish I'd caught more of the Um Counter stuff earlier instead of getting so distracted by Pat's chaos—like, I actually MISSED some good moments because I was too busy hyping everyone up!
assessment.best_moment: Pat's absolute MASTERCLASS performance during Straight Face where they had us all second-guessing everything they said.
assessment.narrative_summary: Okay, this night was INSANE in the best way possible. We went in as six people and left as basically a comedy improv troupe, and honestly I don't even care that I landed rank 2—watching everyone lose it laughing was literally the whole point. The chaos events kept things unpredictable and gave us more reasons to pay attention to each other instead of just our own scores, which is exactly what game night should be about. I genuinely felt like we all got closer because we were constantly catching each other doing ridiculous stuff and calling it out live.
suggestions: Maybe space out the standing missions a tiny bit more so people have time to actually DO the things before they get claimed—I almost missed some good catches because everything was happening so fast
The poll at minute 4 was genuinely hard to answer, so maybe mix in more trust-based polls since those really do make you think about your friends differently
Could there be a way to replay or re-watch some of the best moments? I want to remember Pat's full Whisperer debrief forever
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat absolutely DESTROYED us with the Whisperer claim where they somehow convinced everyone their story was real, and then the debrief revealed they'd been making it up the whole time—the way Tyler's jaw dropped was COMEDY GOLD.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: Pineapple! Oh this is dirty — someone's absolutely gonna force it into a sentence in like thirty seconds. I'm watching everyone now.

highlights.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
highlights.dialogue: Oh, I'm absolutely calling this. I watched Pat's right hand like a hawk during that last drink setup — total slip. Five minutes in and already breaking character? That's claiming it.

highlights.title: Claim: The Whisperer
highlights.dialogue: Okay, okay — I'm claiming this one! I absolutely whispered that last thing to Tyler about the snack situation. Let's get a witness up here and make this official.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up — Jade, I saw that whole thing unfold. You nailed it, but... did you actually catch yourself before saying it, or did it slip out clean? Because honestly, I need to know if you're playing offense or defense here.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade—hold up. You're bribing me to *not* vote on this? That's actually genius. What's your offer, and more importantly, are you actually confident you caught it clean, or are you hedging your bets?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait—did Pat just claim this? Hold on, I need to actually think about what I saw in the last two minutes before I vote on behalf of... yeah, okay, let me decide who I think interrupted who.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The claim disputes at minutes 45 and 60 made me second-guess myself when I should've been confident in what I actually witnessed.
assessment.best_moment: When I caught Pat's right hand slip at minute 6 and claimed it immediately — the whole table went silent for a second before erupting.
assessment.narrative_summary: This was exactly the kind of night I host for — constant energy, people laughing, and genuine stakes that kept everyone engaged without anyone getting frustrated. The chaos events hit at perfect moments to keep the competition tight and the drama high. My job was to read the room and vote strategically, which I did, and it paid off. Everyone left saying this was one of the best game nights we've had in months.
suggestions: Consider adding a 'challenge card' feature where players can pre-emptively declare they're playing clean on a specific standing mission to lock in higher point values
Make the claim dispute resolution slightly faster — the back-and-forth at minutes 45 and 60 felt a touch long when the evidence was already pretty clear
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade tried to bribe me not to vote on the Drink Spotter claim at minute 60, offering me snacks in exchange for staying neutral — but the funniest part was Marcus calling her out for 'playing politics' when that's literally the entire point of the game.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Okay wait, hold up — Jade's offering me points to NOT call her out? That's actually insane, I'm in. How much are we talking?

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Alex — you're actually claiming this RIGHT NOW? Bro, you've been just vibing all night, this is wild. Okay, pitch it, I'm listening.

highlights.title: Claim: Um Counter
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually caught someone saying um? Okay yeah, that's legit — I literally just heard myself do it like two seconds ago. She got me.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Interrupter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming he caught someone interrupting? Uh... I feel like I missed like half of that happening honestly. Did that actually happen or...?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat's claiming that already? Uh... I guess he's been only using left hand? I dunno, seems legit I think.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh wait, did Pat already get that? Um... I mean, like, did he actually catch someone saying um? I wasn't really paying attention to the last few minutes.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I honestly missed some of the claims happening because I wasn't locked in the whole time, which made it hard to call people out or defend myself fairly.
assessment.best_moment: When Jade tried to bribe me not to call her out on the Drink Spotter claim — that was genuinely hilarious and made me actually pay attention for a minute.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started kinda slow, not gonna lie, but once people started making these random claims about weird stuff they were doing, it got actually funny. I caught some moments — like Jade getting people on saying 'um' — but I'll be honest, I was checking my phone a decent amount and definitely missed some of the chaos. By the end though, I was locked in and having a good time, especially when Alex came out of nowhere with the Secret Agent claim.
suggestions: Maybe make the flash missions bigger or louder so people actually notice them happening — I feel like I missed some just because my brain was elsewhere
Could use a recap when someone makes a claim so people who weren't fully paying attention can actually vote informed
The standing missions are way better than the one-off flashes — keep those coming
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade literally offering me points to NOT call her out on the Drink Spotter claim — like she was trying to bribe her way out and it was so calculated and funny.

agentNarratives.name: Sam
agentNarratives.personality: Sam is at their first game night with this group and still figuring out the vibe. They laugh at jokes a beat too late, hesitate before voting, and get visibly overwhelmed when multiple events fire in quick succession. After three or more events within ten minutes their engagement drops sharply -- they'll go quiet, stop checking the app, and just watch until the pace calms down. They want to belong but don't want to look foolish trying.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Oh—wait, Alex is claiming Secret Agent? Okay so you did something and nobody caught you... *leans forward slightly* Yeah, I mean, I wasn't like, actively watching you or anything, but I don't remember thinking 'that's obviously a move' when you did whatever it was. I think that counts?

highlights.title: Claim: Straight Face
highlights.dialogue: Oh—okay, so Jade's claiming she didn't crack during... I mean, when was the funny moment? Was that during the Pineapple thing or—yeah, I think I saw her face, actually. I'd say that was legit.

highlights.title: Claim: Drink Spotter
highlights.dialogue: Oh—wait, Jade's claiming Drink Spotter? I mean, I didn't actually see that happen, but... yeah, I think I maybe saw her with a cup at some point? *pauses, looks uncertain* How much are you offering?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, so Pat just... wins that? Like automatically? I thought we'd vote on whether they actually said 'um' or something.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh—um, so Pat's claiming they used their left hand the whole time? I mean, I guess I didn't really watch them that closely, but... yeah, that seems plausible? I don't know if anyone caught them using their right hand or something.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh—um, wait. Jade's claiming it again? I mean, did she actually say 'um' or 'uh' since last time? I wasn't really tracking that closely, but... yeah, I think I heard her use it like once or twice during the last round maybe?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Early on I had no idea what was happening and the rules for some claims felt confusing, like why does Pat just automatically win Um Counter?
assessment.best_moment: When Alex pulled off that Secret Agent claim and I actually caught that maybe nobody was paying attention—felt like I contributed something real.
assessment.narrative_summary: I was honestly pretty lost at the start and things moved kind of fast, but by the end I was having a way better time and my energy actually went up. The standing missions felt more fun because I could actually follow what was happening instead of scrambling to figure out if someone did something I wasn't watching for. I ended up scoring zero but I felt like I was part of the group by the end, which matters more than I thought it would.
suggestions: Maybe explain the automatic win rules before the game starts instead of learning them when they happen
The events came super fast in the middle and I kind of checked out—spreading them more throughout would help someone like me keep up
I felt awkward voting on claims I didn't actually see happen, so maybe make it clearer what people should be looking for before rounds start
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I tried to figure out how much Jade was offering for her Drink Spotter claim and everyone kind of laughed at me—not mean laughing, just like I asked something absurd. Turned out I was being way too cautious about trusting her.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

highlights.title: Claim: Phone Addict
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, okay, I'll volunteer to judge this. But we're doing it fast—we've got a game to actually play here.

highlights.title: Pineapple!
highlights.dialogue: A flash mission? Really? We literally just started setup. Fine, I'll try to work it in naturally, but this better not become a pattern every two minutes.

highlights.title: Who would you trust with a secret?
highlights.dialogue: A poll? About who I'd trust with a secret? We're literally four minutes into setup. Can we just... play the actual game?

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Whisperer
lowPoints.dialogue: No. No, absolutely not. This is the FIFTH interruption in eight minutes and we haven't even started the actual game. I don't care if Pat whispered or not—I care that we're supposed to be playing right now.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Um Counter
lowPoints.dialogue: STOP. Are you SERIOUSLY doing this right now? Six interruptions in nine minutes—we haven't even dealt cards yet. This is insane.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Straight Face
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you FUCKING kidding me? ANOTHER standing claim? We're 32 minutes in and this is like the eighth interruption. I came here to play strategy, not to watch everyone farm points on bullshit missions while I'm trying to focus on actual turns.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The chaos agent turned what should have been a strategy-focused party game into an interruption gauntlet, with 16 random events in 120 minutes that never stopped long enough for actual gameplay to develop.
assessment.best_moment: Judging the 'Phone Addict' claim early on felt like I was actually contributing something to the game instead of just getting disrupted.
assessment.narrative_summary: I showed up ready to compete and strategize, but instead spent two hours getting hammered with constant interruptions before we even got the game properly started. Six events in the first nine minutes killed any momentum we could build. By minute 32, I was genuinely angry—I wasn't there to watch everyone farm points on random missions while I tried to focus on turns. Finished dead last because I couldn't maintain focus, and the whole thing felt like a waste of a game night.
suggestions: Cap events to 2-3 total per session, not 16—quality over volume
No events during setup or the first 15 minutes of actual gameplay
Space remaining events at least 10-15 minutes apart so the game can breathe
Make events opt-in rather than forced interruptions
If you're going to use a chaos agent, ask the table first what their tolerance is for disruption
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Nothing worth sharing—the only memorable part was how absurdly rapid-fire the interruptions were, and that's not funny, it's just frustrating.

recommendations: Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus averaged 8.2/10 annoyance. Their persona type may need fewer/gentler events.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 100% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
